assumptions_to_verify: Production MCP continues synchronous cursor_runner invocation on the main thread
Operators who want no fallback keep models.openai_api_key empty in config
contradictions_checked: Ambient OPENAI_API_KEY cannot activate fallback — _structured_fallback_available uses request.openai_api_key only
Contract-unmet does not fall through to LiteLLM — _fallback_or_primary_failure short-circuits on non-infrastructure classifications
litellm_structured direct mode may still read ambient OPENAI_API_KEY inside _run_litellm_structured — distinct from fallback activation, consistent with ADR
missing_evidence: Explicit test: cursor_sdk contract-miss with openai_api_key set must not attempt fallback
Full 547-test dev suite not independently re-run in this review (reviewer-focused 80-test run passed)
critical_review.strongest_objection: SIGALRM-based timeout is inert off the main thread and on non-Unix platforms, so hung Cursor SDK calls may not classify as reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable in those contexts.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Failing pytest re-run, reviewer moved to a worker thread/executor, or a test showing contract-miss with explicit openai_api_key incorrectly attempts fallback.

reviewer_contract_unmet preserved; fallback only on reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable.

claims: Structured fallback activates only with an explicit request/config openai_api_key; ambient env cannot activate it.
reviewer_contract_unmet is preserved as contract-unmet; fallback fires only on reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable.
Deterministic coverage exists for env isolation, positive Codex MCP Cursor key load, explicit LiteLLM lower-assurance labeling, and both-runtimes-fail recovery.

Default reviewer_output_mode flipped to cursor_sdk across config.py, the request dataclass, and _reviewer_output_mode_config; cursor_review renamed/dual-written as independent_reviewer with legacy_requirement_id retained.
confidence_criteria: Fallback gate requires explicit key (verified in source).
Contract-unmet preserved, fallback only on infrastructure_unavailable (verified in source).
Four claimed coverages present and assertions match implementation (verified in test source).
Full suite green (documented in test-evidence.md, not independently re-run).
Timeout watchdog robust across execution contexts (NOT verified — main-thread/Unix only).
outcome.confidence_rationale: Implementation logic and all four claimed test coverages were verified by direct static trace of the diffs and they match the asserted behavior, and documented test-evidence reports a green full suite plus live probes. Confidence is held below 0.9 because I could not independently re-execute pytest in this session (interactive approval gate) and because the SIGALRM timeout watchdog is a no-op off the main thread, leaving the timeout-classification guarantee unverified in the real threaded async server context.

assumptions_to_verify: test-evidence.md accurately reflects an actual green run of the full suite and live probes.
invoke_cursor_agent executes on the main thread in the production MCP workflow so the timeout watchdog is effective.
No other call sites depend on the removed/renamed cursor_review requirement_id without the legacy_requirement_id shim.
contradictions_checked: Intent says fallback must require explicit key — confirmed _structured_fallback_available gates on request.openai_api_key, not os.environ.
Intent says preserve contract-unmet — confirmed _fallback_or_primary_failure short-circuits for non-infrastructure classifications.

Verified the broader diff (default flip to cursor_sdk, cursor_review->independent_reviewer) is consistent across config.py, dataclass, _reviewer_output_mode_config, and mcp_tools, with legacy ids retained for back-compat — no contradiction with the stated intent or task scope.
missing_evidence: Independent local re-execution of pytest (blocked by interactive approval); current test_status rests on test-evidence.md plus static trace.
Confirmation of which thread invoke_cursor_agent runs on inside the live MCP async workflow, which determines whether the timeout watchdog is active in production.
Direct inspection of the untracked docs/dual-agent/.../live-cursor-sdk-probe-postpatch summary.json artifacts (probe results read only via test-evidence.md).
critical_review.strongest_objection: The SIGALRM-based _cursor_sdk_timeout watchdog silently becomes a no-op when not running on the main thread or when SIGALRM/setitimer are unavailable; if the MCP server invokes the reviewer from a worker thread, a hung Cursor SDK call would not be bounded and would not be classified as reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable, weakening the timeout->fallback/recovery guarantee in production.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that the reviewer is invoked off the main thread in the live server (making the timeout watchdog inert there), or a failing re-run of the cited tests, would move this from accept to revise.

objections: Residual: _cursor_sdk_timeout watchdog is a no-op when not on the main thread or when SIGALRM/setitimer are unavailable, so the timeout->infrastructure_unavailable classification may not hold inside a threaded async MCP execution context; other failure paths (exception, ModuleNotFound, contract miss) still classify correctly and the watchdog degrades to a safe no-op.
Evidence: pytest required interactive approval that was not granted this session; test_status relies on test-evidence.md plus static trace, not independent re-execution.

specialists.name: lead-static-review
specialists.objection: SIGALRM timeout watchdog no-ops off the main thread / non-Unix; could not independently re-run pytest (approval gate).

outcome.summary: Outcome_review for the post-tdd_review continuation. Verified the central fix: structured fallback now requires an explicit request/config openai_api_key (ambient OPENAI_API_KEY cannot activate it), reviewer_contract_unmet is preserved (fallback only triggers on reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable), and all four claimed deterministic coverages exist (env isolation, positive Codex MCP Cursor key load, explicit LiteLLM lower-assurance labeling, both-runtimes-fail recovery). Static code trace confirms behavior matches the tests; relied on documented test-evidence rather than independent pytest execution.

claims: Structured fallback activates only with an explicit request/config openai_api_key; ambient env cannot activate it.
reviewer_contract_unmet is preserved as contract-unmet; fallback fires only on reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable.
Deterministic coverage exists for env isolation, positive Codex MCP Cursor key load, explicit LiteLLM lower-assurance labeling, and both-runtimes-fail recovery.

Default reviewer_output_mode flipped to cursor_sdk across config.py, the request dataclass, and _reviewer_output_mode_config; cursor_review renamed/dual-written as independent_reviewer with legacy_requirement_id retained.
